steps.title: Identify Text 1's complaint about the genre label
steps.content: Text 1 argues that The Known World, while rich and complex, transcends the “historical fiction” label because that label implies shallow “mere re-creation.”

steps.title: Apply Text 2's defense of the genre
steps.content:
Text 2 argues that historical fiction can explore profound themes and isn't inherently shallow—the genre is misunderstood by critics.

Text 2 would respond that Text 1's dissatisfaction with the genre label reflects a misunderstanding of what historical fiction can achieve.

A and C misrepresent Text 2's position; B incorrectly focuses on the novel's underappreciation rather than the label itself.

That matches choice D—D is correct.
